definition: Careful heedfulness that keeps practice from drifting into neglect or carelessness.
notes: The project prefers heedfulness because it captures the vigilant, non-negligent quality of appamāda without confusing it with sati. Diligence can work in more general prose, but as a default it loses some of the warning against heedlessness built into the term.

context_rules.context: In most ethical and practical contexts
In prose emphasizing careful steadiness in practice
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the line benefits from plainer English.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose highlighting sustained application
context_rules.notes: Use when the practical effort dimension is central.

example_phrases.notes: Classic statement of the term's importance.

example_phrases.translation: heedlessness is the path of death
